50.reason: [This is an assignment (n=5) instead of a comparison (n==5), which will always evaluate to true and modify the value of n, causing incorrect behavior.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 1.0 , reason:  [This is an assignment (n=5) instead of a comparison (n==5), which will always evaluate to true and modify the value of n, causing incorrect behavior.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 1.0 , reason:  [Used assignment operator '=' instead of equality operator '==', causing unintended assignment and always evaluating to true]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 1.0 , reason:  [Using assignment operator '=' instead of equality operator '==' causes the condition to always evaluate to true (since 5 is non-zero), and also modifies the value of n to 5. This leads to incorrect behavior for any input other than 5, and even for n=5 it may cause unintended side effects.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 1.0 , reason:  [This is an assignment (=) instead of a comparison (==). It sets n to 5 and always evaluates to true, causing incorrect behavior for any input not equal to 5.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 1.0 , reason:  [Using assignment operator '=' instead of comparison operator '==' causes n to be set to 5 and the condition always evaluates to true (non-zero), leading to incorrect logic and potentially undefined behavior for other values of n.]
